title: Simulation and rendering are separate clocks
description: Game logic advances in discrete simulation ticks while rendering draws as fast as it can; conflating them ties physics to frame rate.
body: Use when reasoning about loop structure. The simulation should advance in fixed, discrete ticks (deterministic, frame-rate independent) while rendering presents the latest interpolated state at whatever rate the display allows; they are two clocks, not one. Do not advance physics by the render delta. Actions: keep a fixed-dt update and interpolate visuals between sim states. Validation signals include consistent gameplay at any frame rate and smooth visuals via interpolation. Risk boundary: binding simulation to the render rate makes behavior depend on hardware speed and breaks determinism, replays, and multiplayer sync — the decoupling is foundational, not optional.
